The '''habitual aspect''' in Aelaki is the present-tense realization of the [[Atelic|atelic perfect]] aspect. While the atelic perfect in past tense denotes the present result of an unbounded action, in present tense it expresses habitual or repeated actions.

== Formation ==

The habitual uses the same stem modification as the atelic perfect: the '''o-grade''' vowel pattern.

Using the root ''zh-r-n'' "live" (base form: ''zharan''):

{| class="wikitable"
! Aspect !! Form !! Meaning
|-
| Base || zharan || (someone) lives
|-
| Habitual (present) || zhoron || lives (habitually)
|-
| Atelic perfect (past) || zhoron || is alive (state achieved)
|}

The form ''zhoron'' is identical in both cases; the habitual or atelic perfect reading depends on tense context.

== Relationship to other aspects ==

In the present tense, the Aelaki aspect system maps as follows:

{| class="wikitable"
! Past aspect !! Present realization
|-
| [[Atelic|Atelic perfect]] || '''Habitual'''
|-
| [[Continuous|Continuative]] || [[Continuous|Continuative]] (unchanged)
|-
| [[Telic|Telic perfect]] || [[Gnomic]]
|}

== Stative verbs ==

For [[Stative_verbs|stative verbs]], the habitual/[[Gnomic|gnomic]] distinction is maintained. The simple present base form serves as the habitual (e.g., ''zoduk'' "know/knows regularly"), while the umlaut form is strictly gnomic (e.g., ''zedik'' "universally known").

== See also ==

* [[Atelic]]
* [[Gnomic]]
* [[Perfect]]
* [[Stative_verbs]]

[[Category:grammatical aspects]]
